headline: Dewan Rakyat has no code of ethics — 4th attempt to draft one failed
context: The fourth attempt to establish a parliamentary code of ethics for Dewan Rakyat members failed in 2025 when the drafting committee dissolved without producing a final document.

cards.big: 4th attempt at parliamentary ethics code failed — MPs still have no conduct rules
cards.sub: Dewan Rakyat members operate without any formal ethical standards or enforcement

cards.big: Standing Orders contain decorum rules but no ethics framework for conduct outside chamber
cards.sub: MPs face no sanctions for conflicts of interest, undisclosed lobbying, or misuse of position

cards.big: Drafting committee's RM1.8 million budget produced 3 draft codes — all rejected by caucuses
cards.sub: Each political bloc vetoed provisions that would constrain their own members

cards.big: 132 of 148 Commonwealth parliaments have adopted formal codes of conduct
cards.sub: Malaysia is among the 11% of Commonwealth legislatures without basic ethics rules

cards.big: MPs who refuse to write their own ethics code are telling you what they think about ethics
Should a parliamentary ethics code be imposed by legislation rather than left to MPs' self-regulation?
